🔬 Segment-Specific Prescriptions
💎 BOFU — Bottom of Funnel (Highest Value)
▼

Why they're your #1 asset: BOFU audiences consistently deliver 40–60% open rates across Elevate, NPI, and PC Regionals — 2–3x your full-list average. Every missed send to BOFU is revenue left on the table.

✅ Elevate BOFU Rate Day-Of: 59.89% open · NPI Women's Mental Health BOFU: 50.68% · NPI KBYG Registrants: 41.88%

📌 Prescription: Send BOFU 72 hrs before ANY rate change. Include specific dollar savings ("Save $200 before midnight"). Add a P.S. reinforcing the deadline. Never send generic content to BOFU — they know the event.

🎓 Registrants — Most Engaged, Most Under-Served
▼

The surprise segment: Registrants deliver your highest click rates — NPI Reg KBYG Reminder: 18.3% click. Elevate Scholarship Accepted: 40% click rate. These people are committed and want logistics, content, and validation.

✅ PAI Post-Event Survey: 57.3% open · 35.4% click · PCR Day 1 Morning Flash: 60.9% open · 37.5% click

📌 Prescription: Build a mandatory Registrant Welcome Sequence: Day 0 (confirmation + hotel), Day 7 (Career Fair), Day 30 (session planning), Day 60 (KBYG preview), Day 7 before (KBYG full), Day 3 (logistics), Day 0 (welcome). 8 touchpoints minimum.

📧 TOFU — Top of Funnel (Volume Driver)
▼

The awareness engine: TOFU sends (60K–245K+ for PC2026) average 7–15% opens. Faculty personal letters dramatically outperform generic promo copy (Brittany Albright Letter: 15.4% vs 8–9% average).

✅ Send faculty personal letters to TOFU — not registration CTAs. The clinician speaking to the clinician pulls conversion to MOFU.

📌 Prescription: Reserve hard-sell CTAs for MOFU/BOFU. Test "A note from Dr. [Name]" subject lines vs. event-feature subjects. Single CTA: faculty bio or session detail page.

🏆 Alumni — Hidden Revenue Source
▼

Massively underutilized: Alumni open at 8–22%, with NPI Alumni Last Chance hitting 21.9%. Past attendees are statistically most likely to buy again. Yet the USPC 2026 reg list is only 1,217 — a fraction of alumni potential.

✅ NPI Alumni Last Chance: 21.9% open · Elevate Scholarship Alumni: 15.4% open

📌 Prescription: Launch "Welcome Back" alumni sequence 4 months pre-event. Use: exclusive early-bird "Alumni Rate," co-chair personal letter, prior-year highlights, referral incentive. Goal: 25–35% alumni conversion before opening to cold list.

💡 Critical Paid Media Recommendations
⚠️
71% Bounce Rate — Email Landing Pages: Email drives 1,999 sessions but 65%+ bounce. Landing pages must match email subject line exactly. Build dedicated campaign landing pages, not homepage redirects.
✅
Google Brand Campaign: 1,684% ROAS: Your Brand campaign ($2,335 spent → $96,640 value, 4,139% ROAS) is your best-performing paid asset. Increase budget to $5,000+/month.
📈
"Psychiatry CME" — Massive Untapped Search: Only 17 impressions. This high-intent term should have thousands of monthly searches. Expand exact match bidding + create a dedicated CME-focused landing page.
🎯
Geo-Target: CO + CA Underperforming: Leads sessions but bounces at 82%+. Run geo-targeted retargeting with New Orleans-specific messaging. Consider state-specific ad copy.
✅
ChatGPT.com: 225 Sessions: Clinicians are searching for psychiatry conferences on AI. Optimize FAQ and content pages for AI search visibility — this channel will compound.
📄 Source: PC2026 Google Ads & Paid Media Master Strategy · Prepared by Michelle Schmidt, HMP Global Performance Marketing · Data: Jul 2025–May 2026
View Brand:
📊 New: Registrant Persona Analysis (April 30, 2026) — 1,158 unique contacts analyzed. Messaging prescriptions below are grounded in real registration data, not assumptions.
👥 Persona-Specific Messaging Strategy — April 2026 Data
57.6%
Advanced Practice Prescribers (APP) — Your Core Audience
667/1,158 registrants. PMHNPs dominate. Lead with practical protocols, prescriber decision support, safety monitoring, and patient-selection guidance. Subject lines: "What your PMHNP patients need from TRD treatment in 2026" beats "Don't miss PC 2026." ANCC CE signal in every email. Scope-appropriate case framing is mandatory.
18.4%
Physician Prescribers / Psychiatrists — Depth Seekers
213/1,158 registrants. 86.4% confirmation rate. Evidence depth, mechanism, guideline fit, long-term outcomes, and safety/tolerability framing. These contacts respond to faculty credentials and clinical rigor signals. "New data on [specific treatment]" outperforms benefit-list copy for this segment. Greg Mattingly and Rakesh Jain faculty letters convert best.
9.6%
Behavioral Health / Counseling — Workflow-Oriented
111/1,158 registrants. 90.1% confirmation rate — the highest of any persona. Connect to therapy workflows, addiction/trauma care, referral collaboration, and patient engagement. These contacts self-select when they see relevance to their scope. Don't send prescribing-heavy emails to this segment.
⚠️ HubSpot Data Gap: 65 Contacts Need Enrichment
28 contacts have no DMD/HCP, NPPES, credential, title, or organization role evidence. 37 more are organization-context-only with no person-level role data. These contacts cannot be properly segmented until their HubSpot records are enriched. Assign to data ops team for manual review and DMD field updates.
1
BOFU Before Full List — Always
Send rate-deadline emails to BOFU 24–72 hrs before the full list sees them. They convert at 2–5x the rate. Give them first access to savings — they feel special, you get better ROI.
2
Never Send BOFU Generic Content
BOFU contacts know you. Subject lines must contain a dollar amount, deadline, or clinical specificity. "Final 48 hours — save $150" beats "Don't miss PC 2026." Every time.
3
The Day-Of Email Is Non-Negotiable
Elevate BOFU Day-Of: 59.89% open rate. That is your single best email asset. Send it every campaign, every rate deadline. No exceptions. It's your highest ROI send.
📬 Sequence Architecture
✅ Registrant Welcome Sequence (8 Emails)
Confirmation → Hotel → Career Fair → KBYG Preview → KBYG Full → Logistics → Day-Before → Day-Of Welcome. Currently missing at full scale — building it is your #1 post-registration lever.
🔄 14-Day Follow-Up Rule
A second contact 14 days after any rate email pulls 50% of the original response at marginal cost. You're already doing T-7 and Day-Of — add T+14 (value-add content for non-openers) to every campaign.
🎯 Engagement-Based Re-Segmentation
After each send, move openers to MOFU, clickers to BOFU. Non-openers after 3 sends → "We miss you" re-engagement with a completely different subject line approach. Don't keep sending to non-engagers.
✍️ Copy Formula — What Works
✅ Faculty-First Subject Lines for TOFU
"A note from Dr. Mattingly" outperforms "PC 2026 Updates." Clinicians trust clinicians. The personal letter format (Brittany Albright: 15.4% OR, Stephanie Kress: 15.2%) consistently beats generic promo.
✅ Clinical Specificity in Body Copy
"ADHD + mood comorbidity" beats "mental health topics." Clinicians filter for relevance instantly. Your best emails name specific conditions, medications, and patient scenarios.
❌ Avoid Subject Line Fatigue
"Last Chance" works once per campaign. After that, rotate to clinical hooks: "What's changing in TRD treatment — New Orleans 2026" pulls fresher opens than "Don't miss your chance."
📈 Growth Levers — Untapped Opportunities
🚀 Cross-Sell Elevate → PC2026
1,037+ Elevate 2026 registrants are warm leads for PC2026. Post-Elevate email within 48 hours of closing. Offer exclusive Elevate-to-PC discount code. Expected: 8–15% conversion.
🚀 PCR → PC2026 Pipeline
PCR registrants (free CE, online) are TOFU for in-person events. After each PCR session, send: "Liked today's session? 40+ more in New Orleans." 200+ warm contacts per session.
🚀 Post-Event On-Demand Nurture
NPI on-demand emails (25.9% and 16.4% OR) show strong engagement. Build for every event: Access notification → Content reminder → CE deadline → Next event preview.
🚀 Alumni Referral Program
NPI Alumni Referral emails prove the model. Build a portfolio-wide referral program: attend + bring a colleague = discount for both. Group discount emails (Elevate Dec 2025: 11.7% OR) show concept resonates.
🧪 A/B Testing Priorities
🔬 Test #1: From Name
"Dr. Greg Mattingly" vs. "Psych Congress" as From Name for TOFU sends. Faculty-named sends likely lift open rate 15–25% based on your letter email data.
🔬 Test #2: Subject — Clinical Hook vs. FOMO
"What's changing in TRD treatment — New Orleans 2026" vs. "3,500 clinicians will be there. Will you?" Both valid — test to your MOFU list and track CTR, not just OR.
🔬 Test #3: Send Day for BOFU
Your BOFU sends skew Tue/Thu. Test Wednesday 8:00 AM — often a dead day that stands out in clinician inboxes precisely because it's unexpected.
🔬 Test #4: P.S. Line
Add a P.S. to every BOFU email: "P.S. — Registration closes at midnight. After that, rates increase by $[X]." The P.S. is the 2nd most-read element in any email. Never skip it.
⚕️ Compliance & Best Practices
⚕️ Audience: Doctors, Psychiatrists, DOs, PAs, NPs, Mental Health Clinicians. Every email must answer: "What will I learn that helps my patients?" Generic event promotion is discarded immediately.
🔒 No medical claims in subject lines. CME/CE language must be accurate. Avoid superlatives ("best," "cure") in clinical contexts. All faculty credentials must be verifiable. Testimonials must be attributed accurately.
📋 CAN-SPAM / GDPR compliance. All emails include unsubscribe. Physical address in footer. Suppress bounces and unsubscribes within 10 business days. Segment by consent type for EU contacts.
🎓 CE/CME accuracy. Always state exact credit hours. Credit type (AMA PRA Category 1, ANCC, AAPA) must be correct per audience. PA Institute: AAPA-approved language. NP Institute: ANCC accreditation where applicable.

Brand:
🟠 HubSpot Private App Token
Not connected
Enter your HubSpot Private App token to pull live per-email open rates, click rates, delivered counts, and bounce rates directly from the Marketing Hub API. Your token is stored in sessionStorage only — it clears when you close this tab and is never sent to any server except HubSpot directly from your browser.
⚠️ Required scopes: Your Private App must have content scope (Marketing Hub API) to retrieve per-email statistics. Campaign-level analytics via MCP do not require a token.
How to get a Private App token: HubSpot → Settings → Integrations → Private Apps → Create Private App → Scopes: crm.objects.marketing_events.read, content → Generate token
📡 Campaign Analytics — Live from HubSpot MCP LIVE
These metrics come directly from HubSpot via MCP and refresh each time this page loads. No token required. Data: sessions, new contacts, influenced contacts per campaign.

🔄 How to Set Up Automatic Dashboard Updates
Option 1: GitHub Actions (Recommended)
The cleanest approach. A scheduled GitHub Action calls the HubSpot Marketing Hub API nightly, generates a fresh email_stats.json, and the dashboard fetches it on load.
Setup (20 minutes):
1. Create .github/workflows/refresh-hs-data.yml in your repo
2. Add your HubSpot token as a GitHub Secret: HUBSPOT_TOKEN
3. The workflow runs daily at 6 AM ET (configurable) and pushes fresh JSON
4. Dashboard auto-fetches the JSON on every page load
Result: Dashboard always shows last-night's HubSpot stats. Zero manual work after setup.
Option 2: HubSpot Workflow → Webhook
Use a HubSpot Workflow to trigger a webhook whenever an email send completes. The webhook calls a serverless function (Vercel/Netlify) that updates the JSON file and triggers a GitHub Pages rebuild.
Setup:
1. HubSpot → Automation → Workflows → Create workflow
2. Trigger: "Email send completed" (Marketing Email property)
3. Action: HTTP request (POST) → your Vercel/Netlify function URL
4. Function fetches new stats and pushes to GitHub
Result: Dashboard updates within minutes of every email send. Near-real-time.
Option 3: HubSpot Custom Coded Action
HubSpot Operations Hub (Professional+) supports Custom Coded Actions in Workflows — Python or Node.js running inside HubSpot. Use this to push stats to an external endpoint on a schedule without any third-party hosting.
Requirements:
✅ Operations Hub Professional or Enterprise
✅ Private App token stored as a Workflow secret
✅ An endpoint to receive the pushed JSON (GitHub raw or S3)
Result: Entirely within HubSpot. No external infrastructure required.
Option 4: Browser-Side Fetch (Current)
The token panel above already implements this. Enter your Private App token, and the dashboard calls the Marketing Hub API directly from your browser each time you open the HubSpot Live Data tab.
Pros/Cons:
✅ Zero infrastructure — works right now
✅ Token never persists beyond your browser session
⚠️ Requires entering token each session (sessionStorage only)
⚠️ CORS may require a proxy for some API endpoints
Best for: Daily manual review sessions before stand-ups or send decisions.
📄 Ready-to-Use GitHub Actions Workflow
.github/workflows/refresh-hs-data.yml 
name: Refresh HubSpot Email Stats

on:
  schedule:
    - cron: '0 11 * * *'   # 6 AM ET daily (11 UTC)
  workflow_dispatch:         # allows manual trigger from GitHub UI

jobs:
  refresh-data:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout repo
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Set up Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm install axios

      - name: Fetch HubSpot email stats
        env:
          HUBSPOT_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.HUBSPOT_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          node << 'EOF'
          const axios = require('axios');
          const fs = require('fs');

          const token = process.env.HUBSPOT_TOKEN;
          const headers = { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}`, 'Content-Type': 'application/json' };

          // Fetch all marketing emails (paginated)
          async function fetchAllEmails() {
            let emails = [];
            let after = undefined;
            do {
              const params = { limit: 100, ...(after && { after }) };
              const res = await axios.get('https://api.hubapi.com/marketing/v3/emails', { headers, params });
              emails = emails.concat(res.data.results || []);
              after = res.data.paging?.next?.after;
            } while (after);
            return emails;
          }

          // Fetch statistics for a single email
          async function fetchEmailStats(emailId) {
            try {
              const res = await axios.get(
                `https://api.hubapi.com/marketing/v3/emails/${emailId}/statistics`,
                { headers, params: { period: 'TOTAL' } }
              );
              return res.data;
            } catch (e) { return null; }
          }

          async function main() {
            console.log('Fetching email list from HubSpot...');
            const emails = await fetchAllEmails();
            console.log(`Found ${emails.length} emails. Fetching stats...`);

            // Only fetch stats for sent emails (sendCompleted)
            const sent = emails.filter(e => e.state === 'SENT' || e.currentlySending);

            const results = [];
            for (const email of sent) {
              const stats = await fetchEmailStats(email.id);
              if (!stats) continue;
              const counters = stats.counters || {};
              const ratios = stats.ratios || {};
              results.push({
                id: email.id,
                name: email.name,
                subject: email.subject,
                sendDate: email.sendDate,
                campaign: email.campaignName || '',
                delivered: counters.delivered || 0,
                sent: counters.sent || 0,
                opens: counters.open || 0,
                clicks: counters.click || 0,
                bounces: (counters.hardBounced || 0) + (counters.softBounced || 0),
                unsubscribes: counters.unsubscribed || 0,
                openRate: ratios.openRate ? (ratios.openRate * 100).toFixed(2) : 0,
                clickRate: ratios.clickRate ? (ratios.clickRate * 100).toFixed(2) : 0,
                bounceRate: ratios.bounceRate ? (ratios.bounceRate * 100).toFixed(2) : 0,
                unsubRate: ratios.unsubscribedRate ? (ratios.unsubscribedRate * 100).toFixed(2) : 0,
                ctor: ratios.clickThroughRate ? (ratios.clickThroughRate * 100).toFixed(2) : 0
              });
              // HubSpot rate limit: 110 requests per 10 seconds
              await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 100));
            }

            // Write output file
            const output = {
              generatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
              totalEmails: results.length,
              emails: results.sort((a, b) => new Date(b.sendDate) - new Date(a.sendDate))
            };
            fs.writeFileSync('email_stats.json', JSON.stringify(output, null, 2));
            console.log(`Written ${results.length} emails to email_stats.json`);
          }

          main().catch(e => { console.error(e); process.exit(1); });
          EOF

      - name: Commit and push updated stats
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
          git add email_stats.json
          git diff --staged --quiet || git commit -m "Auto-update: HubSpot email stats $(date '+%Y-%m-%d %H:%M ET')"
          git push
📋 Dashboard Auto-Fetch Code (Add to Your HTML)
Add to dashboard <script> on page load 
// Auto-fetch HubSpot stats from GitHub Pages JSON
// Place this in your DOMContentLoaded block

async function autoLoadHubSpotStats() {
  const JSON_URL = 'https://mschmidthmp.github.io/Email-Dashboard/email_stats.json';
  try {
    const res = await fetch(JSON_URL);
    if (!res.ok) throw new Error('Stats file not found');
    const data = await res.json();

    // Update KPI strip with live data
    const delivered2026 = data.emails
      .filter(e => e.sendDate && e.sendDate.startsWith('2026'))
      .reduce((sum, e) => sum + (e.delivered || 0), 0);

    const open2026 = data.emails
      .filter(e => e.sendDate && e.sendDate.startsWith('2026') && e.delivered > 0)
      .map(e => parseFloat(e.openRate));
    const avgOpen = open2026.length ? (open2026.reduce((a,b)=>a+b,0)/open2026.length).toFixed(1) : '—';

    document.getElementById('kpi-delivered').textContent =
      (delivered2026 / 1e6).toFixed(1) + 'M';
    document.getElementById('kpi-open').textContent = avgOpen + '%';

    // Show last-updated badge
    const updated = new Date(data.generatedAt);
    const badge = document.createElement('div');
    badge.style = 'font-size:10px;color:#16a34a;background:#dcfce7;padding:2px 8px;border-radius:10px;';
    badge.textContent = '✅ Live data as of ' + updated.toLocaleDateString('en-US', {month:'short',day:'numeric',hour:'2-digit',minute:'2-digit'});
    document.querySelector('.kpis').prepend(badge);

    // Store for HubSpot Live Data tab use
    window.HS_EMAIL_STATS = data;
    console.log(`Loaded ${data.emails.length} email stats from HubSpot (${data.generatedAt})`);

  } catch (e) {
    console.log('HubSpot stats not yet available — using embedded data.');
  }
}

autoLoadHubSpotStats();
⚙️ HubSpot Workflow: Email Completed → Update Dashboard
Step 1: Create the Workflow
HubSpot → Marketing → Email → Click any sent email → Workflows → "Create workflow from this email"
OR: Automation → Workflows → Create workflow → Contact-based → "Email send/open/click"
Step 2: Set Trigger
Enrollment trigger: Marketing Email → Send → is any of → [select your sends]
Or use Contact Property: Last Marketing Email Send Date → is known
Step 3: Add Webhook Action
Actions → Send HTTP request
Method: POST
URL: Your Vercel/Netlify serverless function endpoint
Body: {"trigger":"email_sent","timestamp":"{{now}}"}
Step 4: Serverless Function
Deploy a Vercel function at your webhook URL. On POST, it:
1. Calls /marketing/v3/emails with your Private App token
2. Builds the JSON
3. Commits to GitHub via API
4. GitHub Pages auto-deploys the new JSON
📚 HubSpot API Reference — Marketing Hub Endpoints
EndpointMethodWhat It ReturnsScope RequiredRate Limit
/marketing/v3/emailsGETAll marketing emails: name, subject, state, send date, campaigncontent110/10s
/marketing/v3/emails/{id}/statisticsGETPer-email: delivered, opens, clicks, bounces, unsubscribes, open rate, click rate, CTORcontent110/10s
/marketing/v3/emails/{id}GETSingle email metadata: subject, from name, audience, campaign tagcontent110/10s
/crm/v3/objects/marketing_eventsGETCampaign-level event datacrm.objects.marketing_events.read110/10s
/crm/v3/listsGETHubSpot contact lists: name, size, last updatedcrm.lists.read110/10s
/crm/v3/lists/{listId}/membershipsGETContact IDs in a specific listcrm.lists.read110/10s
/contacts/v1/listsGETLegacy list API with size propertycontacts100/10s
🔑 What Each Method Can Access
🟠 HubSpot MCP (Already Connected)
✅ Campaign-level sessions
✅ Campaign influenced contacts
✅ Campaign new contacts (first/last touch)
✅ CRM object search (contacts, companies, deals)
✅ List membership queries
✅ Contact property reads
❌ Per-email open rates
❌ Per-email click rates
❌ Per-email delivered counts
❌ Per-email bounce/unsubscribe data
🔵 Private App Token (content scope)
✅ Every email ever sent: open rate, click rate, CTOR
✅ Per-email delivered, opens, clicks, bounces
✅ Per-email unsubscribes + spam reports
✅ Per-email send date, subject, from name
✅ A/B test variant performance
✅ List size at time of send
✅ Email state (SENT, DRAFT, SCHEDULED)
✅ Campaign tag per email
📋 Live Segment List Sizes — HubSpot Lists API
To pull live list sizes automatically, add the crm.lists.read scope to your Private App. The GitHub Action above can be extended to also fetch list sizes for your key segments (BOFU, MOFU, TOFU, Reg Lists) and include them in the JSON output.
Connect your HubSpot Private App (with crm.lists.read scope) to see live segment sizes.
Segment NameLive SizeSegment TypeLast UpdatedChange vs Dashboard
